steps.title: Use the colon as a definition signal
steps.content: The sentence reads: “The many elements of subak are ______: they are joined together into a single cohesive unit.” The colon introduces a definition of the blank. Whatever word fills the blank must mean “joined together into a single cohesive unit.”

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘informal’: Means casual or unofficial — describes social tone, not how physical elements relate to each other. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘optional’: Means not required — implies the elements could be omitted, which contradicts “a single cohesive unit.” ✗
  • Choice C — ‘outmoded’: Means outdated or obsolete — the passage emphasizes subak’s rich historical significance, not its obsolescence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘interconnected’: Means joined together with mutual links — directly matches “joined together into a single cohesive unit.” ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
